question: Which English company name appears at the bottom of the screen during the armor workshop scenes? A. A-JAPAN JAPAN B. Marutake Sangyou C. Samurai Armor D. Armor Workshop
What is the file number shown in the text overlay 'The AJJ Movie File No...'?
What is the predominant color of the samurai armor featured in the display and workshop scenes? A. Black B. Dark Blue C. Red D. Silver
What entities are present and what are they doing?
canonical_answer: Red samurai armor stands displayed indoors behind glass near a signboard. An elderly man wearing a striped shirt and apron hammers a curved metal piece on a wooden block. Hands manipulate segmented armor plates laced with orange cord. The man sands a curved wooden piece on a workbench. Tools rest on cluttered shelves in the workshop background.

question: How many hammers are visible when the person is hammering a metal piece?

Did the video feature BOTH a tall vertical white signboard with black calligraphy AND a red-handled tool being used on a mesh of metal rings? A. Only the signboard was shown B. Only the red-handled tool was shown C. Both the signboard and the red-handled tool were shown D. Neither was shown
Where is the light-colored curved wooden piece resting while the elderly man sands it? A. on a wooden block B. behind the glass C. on a wooden workbench D. on wooden shelves
Where is the red samurai armor helmet positioned relative to the hands working on the mesh of metal rings? A. in the background B. in front of the wall C. on a wooden block D. behind the glass
What action is the person in the blue apron performing?
Describe the specific crafting actions performed by the artisans in the workshop scenes, noting the tools and materials involved at each stage.
canonical_answer: At 6s, a person hammers a curved metal piece on a wooden block; at 12s, hands use a red-handled tool on a mesh of metal rings; at 14s, a hand holds a black tool working on red samurai armor; at 18s, an elderly man sands a curved wooden piece on a workbench.

What specific activity do foreign customers primarily use the ordered armor for?
canonical_answer: They use the armor for martial art practice such as Enbu.
